title: Scientific Protocol for H&E staining in Heart and Skeletal Muscle
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dv6fh","text":"Paper abstract: CRISPR-Cas9 gene-editing technology has revolutionised the creation of precise and permanent modifications to DNA, enabling the generation of diverse animal models for investigating potential treatments. Here, we provide a protocol for the use of CRISPR-Cas9 to create murine models of Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD) along with a step-by-step guide for their phenotypic and molecular characterisation. The experimental procedures include CRISPR microinjection of embryos, molecular testing at the DNA, RNA, and protein levels, forelimb grip strength testing, immunostaining and serum creatine kinase (CK) testing. We further provide suggestions for analysis and interpretation of the generated data, as well as the limitations of our approach. These protocols are designed for researchers who intend on generating and using mouse models to study DMD as well as those seeking a detailed framework of phenotyping to contribute to the broader landscape of genetic disorder investigations.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2icff","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"81kog","text":"Protocol summary: The following protocol describes H\u0026E staining of snap frozen cork-mounted mouse tissue samples. These include sections from the heart, tibialis anterior (TA), triceps and quadriceps.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"698i8","text":"Wear proper PPE (gloves, safety goggles, enclosed shoes and lab coat) and prepare solvents in a chemical fume hood. Dispose used solvents or waste material in an appropriate biohazard waste containers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dkakh","text":"•\tLillie Mayer’s Haematoxylin (0.5%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lmmi","text":"•\tTap water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1fa92","text":"•\t0.1% Eosin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59cht","text":"•\tCoverslip","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2gotu","text":"•\tDPX mountant (Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3aes0","text":"•\tSlide staining tray, rack (ProSci Tech)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Allow the slides to air dry for 5 minutes to eliminate any residual moisture.
100% ethanol for 1 min and repeat in clean 100% ethanol for 1 min.
Xylene for 1 min and repeat in clean xylenes for 1 min. Drain the excess xylenes by tapping the slide on paper towels.
Add one drop of DPX mounting medium to the tissue section and cover with an appropriate-sized coverslip. Drain any excess mounting media. Allow the slide to air dry in a fume hood.
Visualise the stained tissue under a light microscope using bright-field illumination.
Immerse the slide in the following reagents order:
Haematoxylin for 5 min. Drain the excess of the hematoxylin by tapping the slide tray on paper towels
Place slide staining tray into a container and run through tap water for 2-minutes
Eosin stain solution for 3 min. Drain the excess eosin by tapping the slide tray on paper towels
Dip slide tray twice into 70% ethanol
Dip slide tray four times into 95% ethanol
creator: Jayshen Arudkumar
